title: Docker Exec Session Audit
exclusions: Approved exclusions: SRE on-call shell sessions during sanctioned incident response covered by lookups/container_change_windows.csv rows tied to CAB tickets; CI/CD pipeline docker exec invocations that run integration tests in dev clusters routed to a separate dev_oti_containers index; intentionally interactive debug-image containers spawned with documented shell access; kubectl-debug ephemeral debug pods when the Kubernetes runtime still uses Docker as the container runtime and the debug operator is pre-approved; backup-tool docker exec invocations such as pg_dump, mongodump, and mysqldump that classify as business_normal and ride a permanent change window. Does not replace UC-3.1.6 configuration-time privilege posture, UC-3.1.25 docker.sock and 2375 daemon exposure, or UC-3.1.8 engine-process reliability.
evidence: Saved search container_uc_3_1_24_docker_exec_session_audit; lookups docker_exec_command_baseline.csv, container_change_windows.csv, and container_owner.csv versioned in git with weekly CAB-attached commit hashes; weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index with seven-year retention to satisfy PCI DSS Requirement 10 and HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312(b) holds; dashboard drilldowns to raw docker:events exec_create/exec_start/exec_die rows, matching auditd:execve excerpts, and Falco JSON output_fields. External research includes Sysdig Threat Research production case studies on insider-threat exec patterns and credential exfiltration via docker exec; Aqua Security NetCraze and similar campaign reports on cryptominers using docker exec for lateral movement; Falco default rules library on Terminal shell in container, Launch suspicious shell in container, and Spawned process in container; NIST SP 800-190 Section 4.4 on container runtime auditing and Section 4.5 on isolation; PCI Security Standards Council guidance on Requirement 10 in containerized environments.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a disposable Linux host tag a lab image as production via lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv with prod_flag=1 and an empty allowed_command_class_csv, ensure no row in lookups/container_change_windows.csv covers the test minute, run docker exec -i lab-app cat /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token, ingest the resulting docker:events exec_create exec_start exec_die rows into index=oti_containers, execute container_uc_3_1_24_docker_exec_session_audit, and expect critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production with command_class=secret_read, change_window_active=0, and production_tier=1; tear down the lab credential immediately.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Add a row to lookups/container_change_windows.csv whose window_start_epoch and window_end_epoch span the next fifteen minutes for the lab host_id and container_name, tag the lab image so allowed_command_class_csv includes business_normal, run docker exec lab-app mysqladmin ping twice within the window, and confirm the saved search emits only low_business_normal_inside_change_window with change_id and approver populated, no row at any higher tier.

dataSources: index=oti_containers sourcetype=docker:events filtered to type=exec for the three Docker Engine exec lifecycle actions exec_create, exec_start, and exec_die preserving Actor.Attributes.execID, Actor.Attributes.name, Actor.Attributes.image, and the exec_start command argv body so streamstats and stats can compute per-session start-to-die duration plus collapse to one row per host_id container_id exec_id, collected by Splunk Connect for Docker (Splunkbase 4496), Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Docker, or a customer-maintained scripted input running docker events --format json --filter type=exec into HEC; index=oti_containers sourcetype=docker:inspect from a privileged scripted input that flattens Config.Image, Config.Labels.tier, and other environment labels for production_tier inference; index=oti_containers sourcetype=auditd:execve from Splunk Add-on for Linux capturing /var/log/audit/audit.log execve lines keyed on docker_cli for the originating shell account (auid) when an SRE pastes docker exec from a remote terminal; optional index=oti_containers sourcetype=falco:alert from Splunk_TA_falco forwarding Falco JSON for runtime-shell rules such as Terminal shell in container, Launch suspicious shell in container, and Spawned process in container with output_fields preserving container_id, container_image_repository, proc_cmdline, and user_name; governance lookups via subsearch-wrapped inputlookup against lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv keyed on image_key with allowed_command_class_csv, slo_tier, and prod_flag columns plus lookups/container_change_windows.csv keyed on host_id and container_name with window_start_epoch, window_end_epoch, change_id, and approver columns plus lookups/container_owner.csv for owner_team paging routing consistent with sibling UC-3.1.x patterns.
app: Splunk Connect for Docker ([Splunkbase 4496](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4496)) or Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Docker ([Splunk documentation](https://docs.splunk.com/observability/en/gdi/opentelemetry/collector-linux/collector-linux-intro.html)) for docker:events streaming with type=exec preserved and docker:inspect snapshots; Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux ([Splunkbase 833](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)) for audisp-json and auditd:execve transport; optional Splunk_TA_falco to forward Falco JSON over HEC for runtime-shell rule enrichment; customer-maintained docker scripted input acceptable when the vendor pipelines cannot be deployed, provided the same sourcetypes and field shapes are produced.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.1.24 Docker Exec Session Audit. Tunables: index=oti_containers; sourcetypes docker:events (type=exec for exec_create/exec_start/exec_die), docker:inspect (image + tier), falco:alert optional, auditd:execve optional; lookups docker_exec_command_baseline.csv (image_key, allowed_command_class_csv, slo_tier, prod_flag), container_change_windows.csv (host_id, container_name, window_start_epoch, window_end_epoch, change_id, approver), container_owner.csv (host_id, container_name, owner_team); long_session_seconds_warn=120; production tier matches slo_tier prod|tier0|tier_0|mission|gold OR prod_flag=1; earliest=-24h@h latest=@h. Forensic-audit axis distinct from UC-3.1.6 privilege posture and UC-3.1.25 socket exposure.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:events" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval evt_type_lc=lower(toString(coalesce(type, Type, event_type, "")))
      | eval action_lc=lower(toString(coalesce(action, Action, "")))
      | where (evt_type_lc="exec" OR match(action_lc, "^exec_(create|start|die)")) AND match(action_lc, "^exec_")
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, id, Id, actor_id, Actor__ID, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, containerName, Actor__Attributes__name, name, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(Image, image, Actor__Attributes__image, from, "")))
      | eval exec_id=trim(toString(coalesce(exec_id, execID, Actor__Attributes__execID, ExecID, "")))
      | rex field=action "^exec_(?:create|start|die)(?::\s*(?<exec_cmd_extracted>.+))?$"
      | eval phase=case(match(action_lc, "exec_create"), "create", match(action_lc, "exec_start"), "start", match(action_lc, "exec_die"), "die", true(), null())
      | eval command_text_evt=trim(toString(coalesce(exec_cmd_extracted, command, Command, Actor__Attributes__command, exec_command, "")))
      | eval exec_user_evt=toString(coalesce(exec_user, execUser, user_actor, Actor__Attributes__user, http_x_auth_user, http_x_remote_user, kube_user, ""))
      | eval lane="docker_exec_lifecycle"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image_ref exec_id phase command_text_evt exec_user_evt lane ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="auditd:execve" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr, "comm=\"docker\"") OR (match(lr, "exe=.*/docker") AND match(lr, "exec"))
      | eval audit_user=toString(coalesce(auid, AUID, user_actor, USER, user, ""))
      | eval audit_argv_excerpt=substr(_raw, 1, 480)
      | eval container_name="HOST_DOCKER_CLI"
      | eval container_id="host_cli_session"
      | eval image_ref=""
      | eval exec_id=""
      | eval phase="audit_correlate"
      | eval command_text_evt=audit_argv_excerpt
      | eval exec_user_evt=audit_user
      | eval lane="auditd_execve_correlate"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image_ref exec_id phase command_text_evt exec_user_evt lane ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="falco:alert" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(hostname, host, k8s_node_name, dest, "")))
      | eval rule=toString(coalesce(rule, Rule, ruleName, ""))
      | eval rlow=lower(rule)
      | where match(rlow, "shell|terminal|exec|launched in container|attach to container|spawned process")
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerID, k8s_pod_uid, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, k8s_pod_name, containerName, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(container_image_repository, container_image_tag, image, "")))
      | eval exec_id=""
      | eval phase="falco_runtime"
      | eval command_text_evt=toString(coalesce(proc_cmdline, procCmdline, output, evt_arg, ""))
      | eval exec_user_evt=toString(coalesce(user_name, user, userName, ""))
      | eval lane="falco_runtime_shell"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image_ref exec_id phase command_text_evt exec_user_evt lane ]
| eval cmd_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(command_text_evt, "")))
| eval command_class_evt=if(len(cmd_lc)>0, case(
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(^|/| )(bash|sh|zsh|ash|dash|ksh|fish)( |$|;|&)") OR match(cmd_lc, "(?i)python\s*-i ") OR match(cmd_lc, "(?i)nsenter|attach"), "interactive_shell",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)/var/run/secrets/|\.docker/config\.json|/root/\.aws/|\.kube/config|service[-_]?account/token|/etc/shadow|/etc/passwd|aws_secret_access_key"), "secret_read",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(apt|apt-get|yum|dnf|apk|pip3?|gem|npm|cargo) +(install|add|update)") OR match(cmd_lc, "(?i)dpkg +-i"), "package_install",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(curl|wget|nc|ncat|socat|telnet) ") AND match(cmd_lc, "(?i)https?://|//[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}|:\d{2,5}( |$|/)"), "service_command",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(chmod|chown|chattr|useradd|usermod|passwd|ssh-keygen|crontab|chsh) ") OR match(cmd_lc, "(?i)/etc/cron\.|systemctl |update-rc\.d|/etc/sudoers"), "crypto_or_persistence",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(tcpdump|strace|ltrace|gdb|lsof|nmap|netstat|tshark|perf|sysdig) "), "forensic_tool",
    true(), "business_normal"), null())
| sort 0 + host_id, container_id, exec_id, _time
| streamstats current=t global=f min(_time) AS sess_first_ts max(_time) AS sess_last_ts count AS phase_seq BY host_id, container_id, exec_id
| stats min(_time) AS session_start_ts max(_time) AS session_end_ts max(phase_seq) AS phases_observed values(eval(if(phase="start" AND len(command_text_evt)>0, command_text_evt, null()))) AS sess_cmd_mv values(eval(if(phase="audit_correlate", command_text_evt, null()))) AS audit_cmd_mv values(eval(if(phase="falco_runtime", command_text_evt, null()))) AS falco_cmd_mv values(image_ref) AS image_mv values(eval(if(len(exec_user_evt)>0, exec_user_evt, null()))) AS exec_user_mv values(container_name) AS container_name_mv values(lane) AS lane_mv values(eval(if(isnotnull(command_class_evt), command_class_evt, null()))) AS command_class_mv BY host_id, container_id, exec_id
| eval session_command_text=coalesce(mvindex(sess_cmd_mv, 0), mvindex(falco_cmd_mv, 0), mvindex(audit_cmd_mv, 0), "")
| eval image_ref=mvindex(image_mv, 0)
| eval image_key=lower(trim(coalesce(image_ref, "")))
| eval exec_user=coalesce(mvindex(exec_user_mv, 0), "unknown_principal")
| eval container_name=mvindex(container_name_mv, 0)
| eval lanes_seen=mvjoin(lane_mv, "|")
| eval command_class=coalesce(mvindex(command_class_mv, 0), "business_normal")
| eval session_duration_sec=if(isnotnull(session_start_ts) AND isnotnull(session_end_ts) AND session_end_ts>=session_start_ts, round(session_end_ts - session_start_ts, 1), null())
| eval session_id=if(len(trim(exec_id))>0, host_id . "|" . substr(container_id,1,12) . "|" . substr(exec_id,1,12), host_id . "|" . substr(container_id,1,12) . "|" . tostring(session_start_ts))
| eventstats dc(command_class) AS distinct_command_classes_per_image_24h values(command_class) AS command_class_set_per_image BY image_key
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_id [
    search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
    | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
    | eval image_ref_meta=trim(toString(coalesce(Config__Image, image, Image, "")))
    | eval inspect_tier_raw=lower(toString(coalesce(Config__Labels__tier, labels__tier, env_tier, environment, "")))
    | stats latest(image_ref_meta) AS image_ref_meta latest(inspect_tier_raw) AS inspect_tier_raw BY host_id, container_id
  ]
| eval image_ref=if(len(trim(image_ref))==0 AND len(trim(image_ref_meta))>0, image_ref_meta, image_ref)
| eval slo_tier_inspect=lower(trim(coalesce(inspect_tier_raw, "")))
| join type=left max=0 image_key [
    | inputlookup docker_exec_command_baseline.csv
    | eval image_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(image_key, image_ref, image, ""))))
    | eval allowed_class_csv=toString(coalesce(allowed_command_class_csv, allowed_classes, allowlist, ""))
    | eval slo_tier_baseline=lower(toString(coalesce(slo_tier, tier, availability_tier, "")))
    | eval prod_flag=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_production, prod_flag, production, "0")), 10)
    | fields image_key allowed_class_csv slo_tier_baseline prod_flag
  ]
| eval slo_tier=if(len(trim(slo_tier_baseline))>0, slo_tier_baseline, slo_tier_inspect)
| eval production_tier=if(match(slo_tier, "(?i)prod|tier0|tier_0|mission|gold") OR coalesce(prod_flag, 0)==1, 1, 0)
| eval baseline_allows_class=if(len(trim(allowed_class_csv))>0 AND match(lower(allowed_class_csv), "(^|,\s*)" . command_class . "(\s*,|$)"), 1, 0)
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name [
    | inputlookup container_change_windows.csv
    | eval host_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_id, host, ""))))
    | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, container, "")))
    | eval window_start_ts=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(window_start_epoch, window_start_ts, "")), 10)
    | eval window_end_ts=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(window_end_epoch, window_end_ts, "")), 10)
    | eval change_id=toString(coalesce(change_id, ticket_id, cab_id, ""))
    | eval approver=toString(coalesce(approver, approved_by, ""))
    | fields host_id container_name window_start_ts window_end_ts change_id approver
  ]
| eval change_window_active=if(isnotnull(window_start_ts) AND isnotnull(window_end_ts) AND session_start_ts>=window_start_ts AND session_start_ts<=window_end_ts, 1, 0)
| eval long_session=if(isnotnull(session_duration_sec) AND session_duration_sec>=120, 1, 0)
| eval severity=case(
    command_class="secret_read" AND production_tier=1 AND change_window_active=0, "critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production",
    command_class="interactive_shell" AND production_tier=1 AND change_window_active=0 AND long_session=1, "critical_interactive_shell_unsanctioned_production_long_session",
    (command_class="forensic_tool" OR command_class="crypto_or_persistence") AND change_window_active=0, "high_forensic_or_persistence_command_outside_window",
    command_class="package_install" AND production_tier=1, "high_package_install_on_running_production_container",
    command_class="service_command", "medium_service_command_to_external_endpoint",
    command_class="business_normal" AND change_window_active=1 AND baseline_allows_class=1, "low_business_normal_inside_change_window",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity)
| eval recommended_response=case(
    severity="critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production", "Page SOC + platform: docker exec touched a credential/token path on a production-tier container outside any approved window. Preserve exec_create/exec_start/exec_die rows, capture argv and exec_user, rotate reachable secrets, isolate the host, open a PCI/SOC2 incident.",
    severity="critical_interactive_shell_unsanctioned_production_long_session", "Page SOC + service owner: long-lived interactive shell on production tier without CAB approval. Identify principal via exec_user, audit auditd:execve for the calling docker CLI, force re-auth, capture argv history before terminating.",
    severity="high_forensic_or_persistence_command_outside_window", "Forensic/persistence command outside an approved window: capture argv and parent process, validate intent with exec_user owner, require an after-the-fact CAB ticket within 24h or escalate to insider-threat workflow.",
    severity="high_package_install_on_running_production_container", "Package install on a running production container is config drift: rebuild through CI/CD instead, document why the in-place change occurred, add the command to the deploy-pipeline backlog.",
    severity="medium_service_command_to_external_endpoint", "Outbound service command (curl/wget/nc): validate destination against approved registries, capture URL and exit code, confirm no credential exfiltration before closing.",
    severity="low_business_normal_inside_change_window", "Routine business-normal command inside a CAB-approved window: archive to evidence index for PCI Requirement 10 quarterly sample; no page required.",
    true(), "Correlate docker:events exec lifecycle, docker:inspect tier, Falco runtime shell alerts, and auditd execve before closure.")
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name [
    | inputlookup container_owner.csv
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host_id, host, "")))
    | eval container_name=toString(container_name)
    | eval owner_team=toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, ""))
    | fields host_id container_name owner_team
  ]
| table session_id container_id container_name image_ref host_id exec_user command_class command_text session_duration_sec change_window_active production_tier baseline_allows_class severity recommended_response owner_team change_id approver slo_tier distinct_command_classes_per_image_24h
description: Runtime-activity forensic-audit axis on Docker Engine fleets where a docker exec session into a running production container is the moment human authorization meets cryptographic credentials in process memory. This control correlates three telemetry feeds: Docker Engine docker:events with type=exec emitting exec_create, exec_start, and exec_die per session keyed by Actor.Attributes.execID for full session lifecycle and start-to-die duration math; docker:inspect snapshots that supply image and tier metadata so production-tier inference does not depend on operator memory; and host-side auditd:execve plus optional Falco runtime-shell alerts that name the calling principal and corroborate the syscall layer. The pipeline classifies each session into one of seven command_class buckets — interactive_shell, secret_read, package_install, service_command, crypto_or_persistence, forensic_tool, or business_normal — joins lookups for per-image baselines and CAB-approved change windows, and emits six severity tiers ranging from critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production down to low_business_normal_inside_change_window so a Fortune 500 SOC can differentiate a three-AM SRE shell from an insider-threat exfiltration in the same telemetry stream. Unlike UC-3.1.6 which proves configuration-time privilege posture and UC-3.1.25 which proves daemon-level socket exposure, this UC instruments the runtime-activity layer that those configuration-time UCs cannot see, producing PCI DSS Requirement 10 audit evidence with timestamped, immutable rows.
value: Quantifiable benefit shows up as defensible PCI DSS Requirement 10 audit evidence covering individual access to cardholder-data systems, SOC 2 Common Criteria CC6.1 and CC7.2 walkthroughs satisfied with timestamped session rows instead of spreadsheet attestations, and HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312(b) audit-control reviews closed in a single quarterly sample. Insider-threat detection improves because the lateral-movement step where attackers docker exec into running containers to harvest service-account tokens, AWS credentials, or kube config files is the same telemetry signature this control classifies as critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production with a named principal and originating CLI host. Forensics accelerates because every alert row carries a full session-replay artifact: session_id, container_id, exec_user, command_class, command_text, session_duration_sec, change_window_active, and the originating auditd argv excerpt, which collapses mean time to investigation from hours to a single collection interval. FinOps benefits when production-access incident response retires per-incident outside counsel retainers in the six-figure range; one prevented insider-threat investigation pays for years of docker:events ingest. Operational maturity gains a leading indicator: weekly counts of low_business_normal_inside_change_window rows trend upward as a platform team adopts CAB discipline, while critical and high tiers trend downward.
implementation: Enable docker:events (type=exec) and docker:inspect into index=oti_containers, route auditd:execve via Splunk Add-on for Linux, optionally add Splunk_TA_falco for runtime-shell rules, publish lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv, lookups/container_change_windows.csv, and lookups/container_owner.csv from CMDB and ticket APIs, save container_uc_3_1_24_docker_exec_session_audit every 5–15 minutes on earliest=-24h@h latest=@h, route critical tiers to a joint SOC/platform bridge, and archive weekly CSV snapshots to a restricted evidence index for PCI/SOC2/HIPAA samples.
visualization: Primary panel: exec-session timeline with one row per session colored by severity tier, drilldown into raw docker:events exec_create / exec_start / exec_die rows and matching auditd:execve excerpts. Secondary: command_class distribution donut for the trailing twenty-four hours showing interactive_shell, secret_read, package_install, service_command, crypto_or_persistence, forensic_tool, and business_normal proportions. Tertiary: long-session-duration scatter plot by image_ref with reference band at one hundred twenty seconds. Quaternary: sanctioned-versus-unsanctioned heatmap by host_id and hour-of-day so leadership can see when production access concentrates.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Endpoint WHERE nodename=Endpoint.Processes (Processes.process="docker" OR Processes.process_path="*/docker") earliest=-24h latest=now BY Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.process_id
| rename Processes.dest AS host_id Processes.user AS exec_user
| where like(lower(Processes.process), "%docker%")
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Endpoint and Change data models on the search tier when docker exec argv and docker daemon argv are normalized into Endpoint.Processes; retain at least ninety days of summaries for quarterly PCI/SOC2/HIPAA control replays that compare runtime exec activity to configuration-time privilege posture from UC-3.1.6 and socket exposure rows from UC-3.1.25.

references.title: Docker Docs — docker container exec CLI reference
CIS Docker Benchmark (community landing — runtime exec auditing controls)
Sysdig — Triaging a malicious Docker container (container session forensics)
Falco Docs — Rules concepts (default rules and runtime shell detection)
Splunk Lantern — Building a SOAR playbook for container enrichment
NIST SP 800-190 — Application Container Security Guide (Section 4.4 runtime auditing)

knownFalsePositives: Health-check tools that exec into containers for binary-level probes (mysqladmin ping, redis-cli PING, pg_isready) when no probe API exists; classify as business_normal in lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv with a permanent change window so observability platforms do not page leadership at the first health probe of the day. Automated backup tools such as pgBackRest, MongoDB backup operators, and MySQL backup runners exec into containers to invoke pg_dump, mongodump, or mysqldump binaries; pre-approve their image_keys and document the expected schedule alongside the change-window CSV. Sanctioned SRE incident-response shells during declared major incidents that have a CAB pre-approval row are recognized when container_change_windows.csv is refreshed within five minutes of the ticket cutting; if the CSV publisher lags, expect transient critical tiers until the lookup catches up. Kubectl-debug ephemeral debug containers when the Kubernetes runtime still uses Docker emit exec_create and exec_start as if they were ordinary docker exec sessions; route those forwarders through a kubectl-debug allowlist or capture the kube_user attribute via Falco proc_cmdline to differentiate cluster-administrator debug from individual exec activity. CI/CD test runners that use docker exec to run integration tests on builder hosts will produce hundreds of business_normal rows per build; route those to a dev index excluded from this saved search rather than raising thresholds globally. Compose project prefixes can break the change-window CSV join when the CSV stores the bare service name; normalize the publisher to include the prefix exactly as docker:events does. Falco rule renames after vendor upgrades may silence the runtime arm; quarterly replay a lab session to confirm the rlow regex still matches. Cron-driven container maintenance tasks that exec into containers on a fixed schedule will fire if not pre-approved; build a per-image scheduled-task allowlist.

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: optional — risk notables when critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production or critical_interactive_shell_unsanctioned_production_long_session rows correlate with rare originating principals, repeated auditd auid drift, or simultaneous UC-3.1.25 socket-exposure hits

premiumApps.name: Splunk SOAR
premiumApps.note: optional — automate filesystem snapshot, auditd preservation, and SOC bridge notification on critical tiers

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the SOC detection lead, the Linux observability owner, and the CAB that approves change tickets. UC-3.1.24 is the runtime-activity forensic-audit axis on Docker Engine fleets: it answers what real principal opened an interactive session inside a running production container, what command executed once the session opened, how long the shell stayed alive, and whether the moment of access is covered by an approved change window. UC-3.1.6 proves configuration-time privilege posture and UC-3.1.25 proves daemon-level socket exposure; neither sees a docker exec session that lands in a workload built without privileged true and without a mounted socket. The runtime-activity layer is where human authorization meets cryptographic credentials in process memory, and this UC instruments that moment with three independent telemetry feeds.

Boundary contract: Docker Engine emits docker:events with type=exec and three lifecycle actions per session — exec_create when a client asks for the session, exec_start when the kernel namespace handoff completes, and exec_die when the kernel reaps the exec process. Each event carries Actor.Attributes.execID for the session token, Actor.Attributes.name for the container, and an exec_start text body that includes the command argv. Confirm your collector preserves all three actions, the execID attribute, and the command body; some collector defaults filter exec_create or strip exec_start argv and that filtering breaks this control. Validate with docker events --filter type=exec on a manager node while ingesting in parallel.

Pair docker:events with docker:inspect snapshots for image and tier metadata. Reuse the inspect feed already deployed for UC-3.1.6 and UC-3.1.13; ensure the scripted input flattens Config.Labels including a tier or environment label so production_tier inference does not depend on operator memory. Where labels are missing, the SPL falls back to slo_tier in lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv.

Add Splunk Add-on for Linux on every Docker host so /var/log/audit/audit.log lines reach index=oti_containers as sourcetype=auditd:execve. Configure the auditd rule -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -F path=/usr/bin/docker -S execve -k docker_cli plus the equivalent /usr/local/bin/docker variant. The audit feed is the only source that captures the calling auid when an SRE pastes docker exec from a remote terminal. Add the optional Splunk_TA_falco when Falco is deployed; default rule libraries include Terminal shell in container, Launch suspicious shell in container, and Spawned process in container.

Governance lookups: lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv carries image_key, allowed_command_class_csv, slo_tier, prod_flag. lookups/container_change_windows.csv encodes CAB-approved windows with host_id, container_name (matching docker:events Actor.Attributes.name including Compose prefixes), window_start_epoch and window_end_epoch as Unix seconds, change_id, and approver — populated from ServiceNow, Jira ServiceDesk, or PagerDuty OpsGenie API on a five-minute cron with fail-closed semantics so legitimate three-AM access is recognized within the next collection cycle; wildcards in container_name are rejected. lookups/container_owner.csv continues the UC-3.1.x pattern.

Risk briefing: production containers hold S3 tokens, secrets-manager rotations, database connection strings, Kubernetes service-account JWTs at /var/run/secrets, and TLS private keys in tmpfs. An attacker pivoting from a hijacked admin to a Docker REST endpoint can read those secrets in seconds; the same docker exec event looks identical when an SRE legitimately debugs an incident. PCI DSS Requirement 10, SOC 2 CC6.1 and CC7.2, and HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312(b) require audit controls for monitored privileged access to systems holding regulated data; this UC produces timestamped, immutable rows that satisfy those asks without spreadsheet attestations.

Differentiation: UC-3.1.1/.2/.3/.13/.22 monitor reliability, capacity, and probe signals; UC-3.1.6 is configuration-time privilege posture; UC-3.1.25 is daemon and socket exposure; UC-3.1.28 is Swarm replica convergence; UC-3.1.8 is engine-process reliability; UC-3.1.14 is networking I/O. None touch the runtime-activity layer where a session opens, executes commands, and closes; UC-3.1.24 owns that layer.

Licensing and privacy: docker:events type=exec is low volume (under ten exec sessions per host per hour). auditd:execve volume is larger; route to a dedicated collector tier and filter host-side on docker_cli. command_text can include credential fragments; redact at the forwarder where regulation requires and align retention with PCI Requirement 10 ten-year forensic holds.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

On every Linux worker running Docker Engine, Mirantis Container Runtime, or an approved enterprise build, enable a docker:events writer that does not filter exec lifecycle actions. With Splunk Connect for Docker, set the Docker Events modular input to subscribe at unix:///var/run/docker.sock with no type filter and validate that exec_create, exec_start, and exec_die rows reach index=oti_containers within a single collection interval. With Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Docker, ensure the docker_events processor preserves Actor attribute keys for execID, name, and image. Custom scripted inputs that run docker events --format json --filter type=exec into HEC are also acceptable; the SPL coalesce list absorbs camelCase and snake_case variants.

Run docker:inspect on a five-to-fifteen-minute cadence on production hosts and faster on CI executors. Flatten Config.Labels.tier and labels__tier so the pipeline reads environment posture without depending on the baseline CSV alone. Document whether docker:events emits the bare service name or the Compose-prefixed name and align lookup keys accordingly.

Configure auditd: /etc/audit/rules.d/docker.rules with the docker_cli execve rule plus a parallel arch=b32 rule when thirty-two-bit binaries remain. Restart auditd, run docker exec ls / on a lab container, and confirm the audit.log row with comm=docker, the correct auid, and key=docker_cli. Splunk Add-on for Linux maps audisp-json output to sourcetype auditd:execve in index=oti_containers.

When Falco is deployed, store HEC tokens in vault and rotate quarterly; pin a maintained Falco policy bundle and review macros each quarter so shell-terminology drift does not silently mask alerts.

Time synchronization is mandatory. session_duration_sec is computed via min and max of _time per host_id, container_id, exec_id; forwarder drift beyond thirty seconds against the Docker daemon clock breaks streamstats ordering. Validate with chronyc tracking on every host.

Pre-save validation queries: index=oti_containers sourcetype=docker:events earliest=-30m action IN (exec_create, exec_start, exec_die) | stats count by action; sourcetype=auditd:execve earliest=-30m comm=docker key=docker_cli | head 5; sourcetype=docker:inspect earliest=-1h Config__Labels__tier=* | dedup container_id | head 10. All three must return results before the saved search is enabled in production.

Expected fields after extraction: host_id, container_id, container_name, image_ref, exec_id, phase (create, start, die, audit_correlate, falco_runtime), command_text_evt, exec_user_evt, lane, plus inspect-derived inspect_tier_raw and image_ref_meta. The SPL collapses to one row per session via stats keyed on host_id, container_id, exec_id and reconstitutes session_command_text, command_class, exec_user, and session_duration_sec from the pre-collapse multi-value sets.

Step 3 — Create search and alert

Save the SPL as saved search container_uc_3_1_24_docker_exec_session_audit with schedule every five minutes during business hours and every fifteen minutes overnight, time range earliest=-24h@h latest=@h so the window captures long-lived shell sessions opened the previous evening and still not closed. Throttle duplicate alerts per session_id for sixty minutes; allow immediate re-fire when severity escalates from medium_service_command_to_external_endpoint to a critical class within the same trailing hour because that pattern often indicates an attacker exploring before exfiltrating.

Pipeline narrative. The opening comment macro is the contract for indexes, sourcetypes, lookup paths, the long-session threshold, the production-tier match, and the explicit complement to UC-3.1.6 and UC-3.1.25. multisearch fans three runtime arms in parallel: the docker:events exec lifecycle arm is authoritative for session timing because Docker Engine itself emits the events; the auditd arm names the operating-system principal who initiated docker exec from a remote terminal; the Falco arm corroborates the syscall side, especially on containerd-only nodes.

command_class evaluates per event because exec_start carries the command body while exec_create and exec_die do not. The case ladder discriminates interactive_shell (login shells, python -i, nsenter, attach), secret_read (service-account tokens, AWS or kube credentials, shadow files), package_install (apt, yum, apk, pip, dpkg drift), service_command (curl, wget, nc to external endpoints), crypto_or_persistence (chmod, chown, useradd, cron edits, sudoers tampering), forensic_tool (tcpdump, strace, gdb, nmap, sysdig), and falls through to business_normal.

streamstats orders per-session events by _time and counts phases per (host_id, container_id, exec_id) so phases_observed approaches three on a clean lifecycle. stats collapses to one row per session and pulls session command text from exec_start, falling back to Falco proc_cmdline and finally to the auditd argv excerpt. session_duration_sec is max(_time)-min(_time) per session and is null when the session is still open at the end of the window. eventstats lifts distinct_command_classes_per_image_24h so an image that suddenly shows four or five command classes in a day signals operational drift or active investigation.

The two inputlookup joins wrap lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv on image_key (baseline_allows_class via comma-aware regex; slo_tier_baseline takes priority over slo_tier_inspect) and lookups/container_change_windows.csv on host_id and container_name (change_window_active=1 when session_start_ts falls between window_start_epoch and window_end_epoch).

Severity tiers (six plus null): critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production when a session touches credential or token paths on a production-tier container outside any approved window; critical_interactive_shell_unsanctioned_production_long_session when a login shell stays open for at least one hundred twenty seconds on a production tier without CAB approval; high_forensic_or_persistence_command_outside_window when forensic or persistence-class commands run outside an approved window; high_package_install_on_running_production_container when apt, yum, or apk runs inside a production-tier container; medium_service_command_to_external_endpoint on outbound curl, wget, or nc; low_business_normal_inside_change_window for evidence retention only (does not page).

The recommended_response case names the exact next steps per tier. The closing inputlookup join adds owner_team. The final table projects nineteen analyst columns including session_id, container_id, container_name, image_ref, host_id, exec_user, command_class, command_text, session_duration_sec, change_window_active, production_tier, baseline_allows_class, severity, recommended_response, owner_team, change_id, approver, slo_tier, and distinct_command_classes_per_image_24h.

Alert actions: route critical tiers to a joint platform/SOC Sev-1 bridge; include recommended_response inline and a deep-link to UC-3.1.6. high tiers open tickets with thirty-minute SLO. medium tiers ticket without paging unless distinct_command_classes_per_image_24h exceeds three. low tiers archive to evidence only.

Fenced SPL for runbooks must match the spl JSON field byte-for-byte:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.1.24 Docker Exec Session Audit. Tunables: index=oti_containers; sourcetypes docker:events (type=exec for exec_create/exec_start/exec_die), docker:inspect (image + tier), falco:alert optional, auditd:execve optional; lookups docker_exec_command_baseline.csv (image_key, allowed_command_class_csv, slo_tier, prod_flag), container_change_windows.csv (host_id, container_name, window_start_epoch, window_end_epoch, change_id, approver), container_owner.csv (host_id, container_name, owner_team); long_session_seconds_warn=120; production tier matches slo_tier prod|tier0|tier_0|mission|gold OR prod_flag=1; earliest=-24h@h latest=@h. Forensic-audit axis distinct from UC-3.1.6 privilege posture and UC-3.1.25 socket exposure.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:events" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval evt_type_lc=lower(toString(coalesce(type, Type, event_type, "")))
      | eval action_lc=lower(toString(coalesce(action, Action, "")))
      | where (evt_type_lc="exec" OR match(action_lc, "^exec_(create|start|die)")) AND match(action_lc, "^exec_")
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, id, Id, actor_id, Actor__ID, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, containerName, Actor__Attributes__name, name, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(Image, image, Actor__Attributes__image, from, "")))
      | eval exec_id=trim(toString(coalesce(exec_id, execID, Actor__Attributes__execID, ExecID, "")))
      | rex field=action "^exec_(?:create|start|die)(?::\s*(?<exec_cmd_extracted>.+))?$"
      | eval phase=case(match(action_lc, "exec_create"), "create", match(action_lc, "exec_start"), "start", match(action_lc, "exec_die"), "die", true(), null())
      | eval command_text_evt=trim(toString(coalesce(exec_cmd_extracted, command, Command, Actor__Attributes__command, exec_command, "")))
      | eval exec_user_evt=toString(coalesce(exec_user, execUser, user_actor, Actor__Attributes__user, http_x_auth_user, http_x_remote_user, kube_user, ""))
      | eval lane="docker_exec_lifecycle"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image_ref exec_id phase command_text_evt exec_user_evt lane ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="auditd:execve" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr, "comm=\"docker\"") OR (match(lr, "exe=.*/docker") AND match(lr, "exec"))
      | eval audit_user=toString(coalesce(auid, AUID, user_actor, USER, user, ""))
      | eval audit_argv_excerpt=substr(_raw, 1, 480)
      | eval container_name="HOST_DOCKER_CLI"
      | eval container_id="host_cli_session"
      | eval image_ref=""
      | eval exec_id=""
      | eval phase="audit_correlate"
      | eval command_text_evt=audit_argv_excerpt
      | eval exec_user_evt=audit_user
      | eval lane="auditd_execve_correlate"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image_ref exec_id phase command_text_evt exec_user_evt lane ]
    [ search index=oti_containers sourcetype="falco:alert" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(hostname, host, k8s_node_name, dest, "")))
      | eval rule=toString(coalesce(rule, Rule, ruleName, ""))
      | eval rlow=lower(rule)
      | where match(rlow, "shell|terminal|exec|launched in container|attach to container|spawned process")
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerID, k8s_pod_uid, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, k8s_pod_name, containerName, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(container_image_repository, container_image_tag, image, "")))
      | eval exec_id=""
      | eval phase="falco_runtime"
      | eval command_text_evt=toString(coalesce(proc_cmdline, procCmdline, output, evt_arg, ""))
      | eval exec_user_evt=toString(coalesce(user_name, user, userName, ""))
      | eval lane="falco_runtime_shell"
      | fields _time host_id container_id container_name image_ref exec_id phase command_text_evt exec_user_evt lane ]
| eval cmd_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(command_text_evt, "")))
| eval command_class_evt=if(len(cmd_lc)>0, case(
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(^|/| )(bash|sh|zsh|ash|dash|ksh|fish)( |$|;|&)") OR match(cmd_lc, "(?i)python\s*-i ") OR match(cmd_lc, "(?i)nsenter|attach"), "interactive_shell",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)/var/run/secrets/|\.docker/config\.json|/root/\.aws/|\.kube/config|service[-_]?account/token|/etc/shadow|/etc/passwd|aws_secret_access_key"), "secret_read",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(apt|apt-get|yum|dnf|apk|pip3?|gem|npm|cargo) +(install|add|update)") OR match(cmd_lc, "(?i)dpkg +-i"), "package_install",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(curl|wget|nc|ncat|socat|telnet) ") AND match(cmd_lc, "(?i)https?://|//[a-z0-9.-]+\.[a-z]{2,}|:\d{2,5}( |$|/)"), "service_command",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(chmod|chown|chattr|useradd|usermod|passwd|ssh-keygen|crontab|chsh) ") OR match(cmd_lc, "(?i)/etc/cron\.|systemctl |update-rc\.d|/etc/sudoers"), "crypto_or_persistence",
    match(cmd_lc, "(?i)(tcpdump|strace|ltrace|gdb|lsof|nmap|netstat|tshark|perf|sysdig) "), "forensic_tool",
    true(), "business_normal"), null())
| sort 0 + host_id, container_id, exec_id, _time
| streamstats current=t global=f min(_time) AS sess_first_ts max(_time) AS sess_last_ts count AS phase_seq BY host_id, container_id, exec_id
| stats min(_time) AS session_start_ts max(_time) AS session_end_ts max(phase_seq) AS phases_observed values(eval(if(phase="start" AND len(command_text_evt)>0, command_text_evt, null()))) AS sess_cmd_mv values(eval(if(phase="audit_correlate", command_text_evt, null()))) AS audit_cmd_mv values(eval(if(phase="falco_runtime", command_text_evt, null()))) AS falco_cmd_mv values(image_ref) AS image_mv values(eval(if(len(exec_user_evt)>0, exec_user_evt, null()))) AS exec_user_mv values(container_name) AS container_name_mv values(lane) AS lane_mv values(eval(if(isnotnull(command_class_evt), command_class_evt, null()))) AS command_class_mv BY host_id, container_id, exec_id
| eval session_command_text=coalesce(mvindex(sess_cmd_mv, 0), mvindex(falco_cmd_mv, 0), mvindex(audit_cmd_mv, 0), "")
| eval image_ref=mvindex(image_mv, 0)
| eval image_key=lower(trim(coalesce(image_ref, "")))
| eval exec_user=coalesce(mvindex(exec_user_mv, 0), "unknown_principal")
| eval container_name=mvindex(container_name_mv, 0)
| eval lanes_seen=mvjoin(lane_mv, "|")
| eval command_class=coalesce(mvindex(command_class_mv, 0), "business_normal")
| eval session_duration_sec=if(isnotnull(session_start_ts) AND isnotnull(session_end_ts) AND session_end_ts>=session_start_ts, round(session_end_ts - session_start_ts, 1), null())
| eval session_id=if(len(trim(exec_id))>0, host_id . "|" . substr(container_id,1,12) . "|" . substr(exec_id,1,12), host_id . "|" . substr(container_id,1,12) . "|" . tostring(session_start_ts))
| eventstats dc(command_class) AS distinct_command_classes_per_image_24h values(command_class) AS command_class_set_per_image BY image_key
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_id [
    search index=oti_containers sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
    | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
    | eval image_ref_meta=trim(toString(coalesce(Config__Image, image, Image, "")))
    | eval inspect_tier_raw=lower(toString(coalesce(Config__Labels__tier, labels__tier, env_tier, environment, "")))
    | stats latest(image_ref_meta) AS image_ref_meta latest(inspect_tier_raw) AS inspect_tier_raw BY host_id, container_id
  ]
| eval image_ref=if(len(trim(image_ref))==0 AND len(trim(image_ref_meta))>0, image_ref_meta, image_ref)
| eval slo_tier_inspect=lower(trim(coalesce(inspect_tier_raw, "")))
| join type=left max=0 image_key [
    | inputlookup docker_exec_command_baseline.csv
    | eval image_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(image_key, image_ref, image, ""))))
    | eval allowed_class_csv=toString(coalesce(allowed_command_class_csv, allowed_classes, allowlist, ""))
    | eval slo_tier_baseline=lower(toString(coalesce(slo_tier, tier, availability_tier, "")))
    | eval prod_flag=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(is_production, prod_flag, production, "0")), 10)
    | fields image_key allowed_class_csv slo_tier_baseline prod_flag
  ]
| eval slo_tier=if(len(trim(slo_tier_baseline))>0, slo_tier_baseline, slo_tier_inspect)
| eval production_tier=if(match(slo_tier, "(?i)prod|tier0|tier_0|mission|gold") OR coalesce(prod_flag, 0)==1, 1, 0)
| eval baseline_allows_class=if(len(trim(allowed_class_csv))>0 AND match(lower(allowed_class_csv), "(^|,\s*)" . command_class . "(\s*,|$)"), 1, 0)
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name [
    | inputlookup container_change_windows.csv
    | eval host_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_id, host, ""))))
    | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, container, "")))
    | eval window_start_ts=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(window_start_epoch, window_start_ts, "")), 10)
    | eval window_end_ts=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(window_end_epoch, window_end_ts, "")), 10)
    | eval change_id=toString(coalesce(change_id, ticket_id, cab_id, ""))
    | eval approver=toString(coalesce(approver, approved_by, ""))
    | fields host_id container_name window_start_ts window_end_ts change_id approver
  ]
| eval change_window_active=if(isnotnull(window_start_ts) AND isnotnull(window_end_ts) AND session_start_ts>=window_start_ts AND session_start_ts<=window_end_ts, 1, 0)
| eval long_session=if(isnotnull(session_duration_sec) AND session_duration_sec>=120, 1, 0)
| eval severity=case(
    command_class="secret_read" AND production_tier=1 AND change_window_active=0, "critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production",
    command_class="interactive_shell" AND production_tier=1 AND change_window_active=0 AND long_session=1, "critical_interactive_shell_unsanctioned_production_long_session",
    (command_class="forensic_tool" OR command_class="crypto_or_persistence") AND change_window_active=0, "high_forensic_or_persistence_command_outside_window",
    command_class="package_install" AND production_tier=1, "high_package_install_on_running_production_container",
    command_class="service_command", "medium_service_command_to_external_endpoint",
    command_class="business_normal" AND change_window_active=1 AND baseline_allows_class=1, "low_business_normal_inside_change_window",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity)
| eval recommended_response=case(
    severity="critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production", "Page SOC + platform: docker exec touched a credential/token path on a production-tier container outside any approved window. Preserve exec_create/exec_start/exec_die rows, capture argv and exec_user, rotate reachable secrets, isolate the host, open a PCI/SOC2 incident.",
    severity="critical_interactive_shell_unsanctioned_production_long_session", "Page SOC + service owner: long-lived interactive shell on production tier without CAB approval. Identify principal via exec_user, audit auditd:execve for the calling docker CLI, force re-auth, capture argv history before terminating.",
    severity="high_forensic_or_persistence_command_outside_window", "Forensic/persistence command outside an approved window: capture argv and parent process, validate intent with exec_user owner, require an after-the-fact CAB ticket within 24h or escalate to insider-threat workflow.",
    severity="high_package_install_on_running_production_container", "Package install on a running production container is config drift: rebuild through CI/CD instead, document why the in-place change occurred, add the command to the deploy-pipeline backlog.",
    severity="medium_service_command_to_external_endpoint", "Outbound service command (curl/wget/nc): validate destination against approved registries, capture URL and exit code, confirm no credential exfiltration before closing.",
    severity="low_business_normal_inside_change_window", "Routine business-normal command inside a CAB-approved window: archive to evidence index for PCI Requirement 10 quarterly sample; no page required.",
    true(), "Correlate docker:events exec lifecycle, docker:inspect tier, Falco runtime shell alerts, and auditd execve before closure.")
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name [
    | inputlookup container_owner.csv
    | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host_id, host, "")))
    | eval container_name=toString(container_name)
    | eval owner_team=toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, ""))
    | fields host_id container_name owner_team
  ]
| table session_id container_id container_name image_ref host_id exec_user command_class command_text session_duration_sec change_window_active production_tier baseline_allows_class severity recommended_response owner_team change_id approver slo_tier distinct_command_classes_per_image_24h
```

Step 4 — Validate

Positive path A — sanctioned interactive shell: load lookups/container_change_windows.csv with a window covering the next ten minutes for a lab container_name, run docker exec -it lab-app bash, exit. Confirm low_business_normal_inside_change_window with change_id and approver populated.

Positive path B — unsanctioned secret read on production tier: tag a lab image production via lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv with prod_flag=1, ensure no covering row in container_change_windows.csv, run docker exec -i lab-app cat /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token. Confirm critical_secret_read_unsanctioned_production with command_class=secret_read and recommended_response naming PCI/SOC2 triage. Tear down lab credentials immediately.

Positive path C — long-lived shell on production tier: leave docker exec -it lab-app bash open for at least 150 seconds, exit. Confirm critical_interactive_shell_unsanctioned_production_long_session with session_duration_sec above 120.

Positive path D — forensic tool outside window: docker exec lab-app tcpdump -i any -c 5 outside any approved window. Confirm high_forensic_or_persistence_command_outside_window.

Positive path E — package install drift: docker exec lab-app apt-get install -y curl on a production-tier container. Confirm high_package_install_on_running_production_container.

Positive path F — outbound service command: docker exec lab-app curl -sS https://example.com. Confirm medium_service_command_to_external_endpoint.

Positive path G — auditd correlation: from a shell account whose auid is recorded, run docker exec lab-app whoami; verify audit_correlate appears in lanes_seen for the same session_id.

Positive path H — Falco corroboration: with Falco deployed, run docker exec -it lab-app sh and confirm a falco:alert row with rule mentioning shell or terminal in container; verify falco_runtime in lanes_seen.

Negative path — sanctioned health-probe loop: docker exec lab-app mysqladmin ping every thirty seconds inside a long maintenance window. Confirm only low_business_normal_inside_change_window because mysqladmin classifies as business_normal and the window is active.

Field sanity: temporarily rename docker:events Actor.Attributes.execID in a sandbox forwarder to mimic camelCase-only payloads and verify exec_id still populates via the coalesce list. RBAC: a reader without index=oti_containers must see zero rows. Performance: run Job Inspector at peak and push docker:events into a five-minute summary index keyed on host_id, container_id, exec_id if scan cost exceeds budget.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — exec_die never arrives for a session: the kernel reaped the exec process but Docker did not emit exec_die because the daemon restarted or the events stream lost its socket. Inspect splunkd.log for events-input gaps, rerun docker events --filter type=exec on the host, tighten poll intervals or switch to a multi-writer collector path. Open sessions remain with phases_observed=2 and session_duration_sec=null; treat that state as a still-open shell, not a parser bug.

Case 2 — Compose project prefixes break the change-window join: docker:events emits container_name with a Compose project prefix while the change-window CSV stores only the bare service name. Normalize the CSV publisher to include the prefix exactly as docker:events does; add an alias row when an SRE swaps stack names mid-incident.

Case 3 — auditd arm does not see the calling user: auid is set at login; if the SRE escalates with sudo -i before docker exec, audit captures the original auid which is correct. If RBAC tools mask auid, route detections via Falco user_name as the secondary attribution path.

Case 4 — Falco rule names changed after a vendor upgrade: the falco arm filters rule strings shell, terminal, exec, launched in container, attach to container, spawned process. After upgrading rule packs, verify a lab session still fires an expected alert and update the rlow regex in the comment macro before muting.

Case 5 — Long-running interactive shells used by service-mesh sidecars or observability agents: occasionally legitimate sidecars hold a persistent shell. Allowlist them in lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv with interactive_shell in allowed_command_class_csv, set prod_flag=1 only when explicitly approved by security, and pair with a permanent change window. Refuse blanket suppression.

Case 6 — Production-tier inference failing because Config.Labels.tier is missing: add the label during base-image promotion or rely on slo_tier in lookups/docker_exec_command_baseline.csv. Document the fallback in the saved-search description.

Case 7 — Change-window CSV stale because the ticketing API is down: build the publisher with a fail-closed posture so a publisher failure leaves the previous CSV intact rather than emitting an empty file. An empty CSV marks every session as outside any approved window and creates a paging storm.

Case 8 — Short test-runner sessions polluting non-production indexes: CI clusters frequently spawn docker exec sessions for integration tests. Route those forwarders to a dev_oti_containers index excluded from the saved-search comment macro, or maintain a separate scheduled search at lower severity.

Case 9 — kubectl-debug ephemeral debug containers: when Kubernetes wraps Docker and kubectl debug creates an ephemeral debug pod, Docker still emits exec_create and exec_start. If governance pre-approves kubectl-debug usage, capture kube_user via Falco or docker:events HTTP X-Auth header propagation and lookup that user against an approved-debug-operators CSV.

Case 10 — Splunk Cloud schedule overlap during search-head autoscaling: a five-minute schedule may overlap and create duplicate rows. Use stats latest by session_id at the alert-action layer to deduplicate, or extend the throttle window to thirty minutes for low and medium tiers.

Dashboard publishing: build an exec-session timeline by user, a command_class distribution donut for the trailing twenty-four hours, a long-session scatter plot by image, and a sanctioned-versus-unsanctioned heatmap by host_id and hour-of-day. Annotate panels with deep links to raw docker:events lifecycle rows, matching auditd:execve excerpts, and Falco proc_cmdline payloads.

Evidence retention: weekly CSV exports of the closing table to a restricted evidence index with lookup commit hashes for docker_exec_command_baseline.csv and container_change_windows.csv satisfy PCI Requirement 10 quarterly samples, SOC 2 CC6.1 and CC7.2 walkthroughs, and HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312(b) audit-control reviews. Pair each export with the matching CAB ticket exports so an auditor can replay a quarter of activity in a single sitting.

Governance: quarterly, replay one historical incident through the SPL after Docker, Falco, or auditd plugin upgrades. Update the comment macro when indexes move. Rotate HEC tokens every ninety days. Track low_business_normal_inside_change_window rows weekly as a leading indicator of CAB-discipline maturity.

Supplemental notes: when rootless Docker becomes default, verify auid attribution still works. When migrating to containerd-only, retire this UC on those node classes; kubelet exec belongs under UC-3.2.x. With Splunk ES, map severity to risk scores keyed on exec_user. With Splunk SOAR, critical tiers can drive a playbook that snapshots the container filesystem, captures matching auditd lines, opens a ticket, and notifies the SOC bridge. When red teaming, pair this UC with UC-3.1.25 and UC-3.1.6 for a complete runtime story. When legal requests litigation holds, include docker:events exec rows, auditd:execve lines, Falco JSON, and the docker:inspect snapshot in preservation scope.
grandmaExplanation: We treat live containers like a bank vault with a security camera inside. When someone opens the door we record who walked in, every thing they touched, and when they walked out, so a quiet three-AM fix and a stranger's secret-grab look completely different in the evidence locker.
hardware: Targets sixty-four-bit Linux workers running Docker CE, Mirantis Container Runtime, or enterprise-hardened Engine builds on fleet SKUs such as Dell PowerEdge R750, HPE ProLiant DL360 Gen11, Lenovo ThinkSystem SR650 V3, and AWS Graviton m7g instances where auditd, Falco BPF probes, and Docker daemon event streams behave identically across architectures. Time synchronization is mandatory because session_duration_sec depends on min and max of _time per host_id, container_id, exec_id; chronyd or ntpd drift beyond thirty seconds must be remediated before alerts are trusted. Edge industrial gateways that still run Docker should follow the same exec auditing cadence; rootless Docker remains in-scope but verify auid attribution because user namespaces shift. Windows containers and pure containerd or CRI-O nodes without dockerd are explicitly out of scope for the default SPL.
